headline: GOLD BREAKS $4,781/oz; UP $1,551 IN ONE YEAR AS DOLLAR HEMORRHAGES
snippet: Gold traded at $4,781 per ounce on April 14, 2026 — up $53 on the day and $1,551 higher year-over-year — as US-Iran ceasefire uncertainty and persistent dollar weakness sustain the historic bull run. At current levels gold has risen roughly 48% in the past 12 months, driven by central bank accumulation and safe-haven demand that shows no sign of abating.

headline: SILVER UP 150% IN ONE YEAR; China Imports Hit 8-Year High as Industrial Demand Rewrites Supply Math
snippet: Silver has surged more than 150% over the past year to levels unseen in over a decade, outpacing gold as solar, EV, and AI data center demand converges with a structural supply deficit. China's silver imports hit 206.76 tonnes in just the first two months of 2026 — an 8-year high — further tightening global inventories at a moment when mine supply cannot respond quickly.

headline: COPPER SMASHES $6/LB, UP 30% Year-Over-Year — Electrification Demand Rewrites Supply Math
snippet: Copper rose to $6.01 per pound on April 14, up 30.61% year-over-year, as structural demand from electric vehicles, data centers, and grid electrification continues to absorb global supply. LME warehouse inventories are at an 8-year high creating near-term noise, but analysts point to a deepening structural deficit that no wave of new mine supply can close through the decade.

headline: Gold's April SURGE Signals Growing Anxiety Over Fiscal and Monetary Stability
snippet: Gold's April 2026 breakout past $4,700 is increasingly being read not as speculative froth but as a structural repricing of US fiscal and monetary credibility, with debt ceiling uncertainty and Fed independence questions converging. The piece argues that $4,000 — once resistance — is now the floor, and the path to $5,000 is clearer than mainstream consensus acknowledges.

headline: ABRASILVER Intercepts 171 Metres at 0.83 g/t Gold at Diablillos, Argentina
snippet: AbraSilver Resource Corp. released new drill results from its Diablillos project in Argentina's Salta province on April 13, highlighted by 171 metres grading 0.83 g/t gold at the JAC zone, confirming grade continuity and deposit scale. The result advances one of Argentina's most closely watched junior development assets as the company targets resource expansion in the ongoing Phase V/VI drill program.

headline: Q-GOLD RESOURCES +103% This Week After Maiden Quartz Mountain PEA Stuns Street
snippet: Q-Gold Resources topped TSX-V gainers this week with a 103% surge after releasing a maiden preliminary economic assessment for its Quartz Mountain project, demonstrating the market's appetite for de-risked junior gold assets during the current bull cycle. The PEA result is a key de-risking milestone that repositions the stock for potential strategic interest from larger operators.

headline: C$3 BILLION Deal Consolidates Guyana's Gold Belt — G Mining Ventures Swallows G2 Goldfields
snippet: G Mining Ventures (GMIN) agreed to acquire G2 Goldfields in a C$3 billion (~US$2.2 billion) deal that consolidates the Oko West and Oko-Ghanie gold projects into a single large-scale hub in Guyana's emerging gold belt. The combined operation is projected to produce more than 500,000 ounces of gold annually with C$275 million in life-of-mine cost savings, making it a top-tier global gold asset.

headline: Asian Metals: Gold, Silver, Platinum Rally Extends Into April 14 Trading Session
snippet: GoldSeek's daily Asian metals market update for April 14, 2026 tracks continued buying across gold, silver, and platinum as geopolitical tailwinds and safe-haven demand sustain momentum from the prior session. The note covers key price levels and Asian trading volume signals that set the tone for North American opens — useful context for retail investors tracking entry points.

headline: GOLD-TO-SILVER RATIO Hits 105 Then Reverses — A Historic Buy Signal for Silver
snippet: The gold-to-silver ratio peaked above 105 in early April 2026 — matching the 2020 COVID panic extreme — before reversing in a contraction that has historically preceded sharp silver outperformance. GoldSeek's SWOT analysis notes that past reversals from such extreme levels have triggered rapid silver catch-up rallies, with the current setup mirroring the 2008-2009 and 2020 patterns closely.

headline: Lithium DOUBLES in Q1 2026; Pilbara, Core Lithium Plot Australian Mine Restarts
snippet: Battery-grade lithium carbonate nearly doubled to US$26,278 per tonne in Q1 2026 as supply delays and speculative buying drove the sharpest recovery from multi-year lows, with spodumene prices breaking back above US$2,000 per metric tonne for the first time since late 2023. Pilbara Minerals is weighing a Ngungaju plant restart while Core Lithium approved a Finniss FID with A$290 million from Glencore — signalling a market turning point.

headline: THE URANIUM RUSH Nobody Is Talking About: NexGen and Denison Both Breaking Ground
snippet: An Investing.com analysis argues that the simultaneous construction approvals for NexGen's Rook I (11.5M kg/year) and Denison's Phoenix uranium projects — the first new Canadian uranium mines in over two decades — represents a structural supply event that mainstream media has dramatically underreported. With first production not arriving until 2028-2030 and spot prices near US$100/lb, the supply-demand imbalance is set to deepen further before any relief arrives.
